result.finding: HTTP is the only inter-service communication protocol used. The application exposes HTTP endpoints via Spring MVC @Controller classes. JDBC (TCP/1521) is used internally for Oracle DB access within the Docker network. No gRPC, WebSocket, or message queue protocols detected.
evidence: HomeController: @Controller with GET / and POST /upload endpoints
DetailController: @Controller with GET /detail/{id} and POST /detail/{id}/delete endpoints
PhotoFileController: @Controller with GET /photo/{id} for BLOB serving
No RabbitMQ, Kafka, or WebSocket dependencies in pom.xml
Oracle DB accessed via JDBC over TCP port 1521 (internal Docker network only)
values: HTTP (port 8080) — primary protocol for client-server communication
Spring MVC @Controller (server-side rendering with Thymeleaf + JSON response for uploads)
JDBC over TCP (port 1521) — internal Oracle DB connection only
No gRPC, no message queues, no WebSocket
